title: Fix Chat.tsx: Route Gemini calls directly via @google/genai SDK instead of ICP canister

requirements.summary: Replace ICP canister-routed Gemini calls in Chat.tsx with direct @google/genai SDK integration using user's stored API key
acceptanceCriteria: Chat.tsx no longer calls any ICP actor method (e.g., sendMessage) for AI responses
Gemini API calls are made directly from the frontend using @google/genai SDK with GoogleGenAI instantiated with the user's geminiApiKey
The IC0508 canister-stopped error no longer appears when sending a chat message
System prompt is dynamically built from the user's timetable slots and tasks
If geminiApiKey is absent from UserContext, an inline message prompts the user to add their key in the sidebar
Chat UI remains intact: scrollable history, Send button, Enter-to-send, typing indicator, Clear Chat button, and formatted responses all work correctly

file_operations.description: Remove any ICP actor method calls for Gemini API and replace with direct @google/genai SDK integration. Import GoogleGenAI from '@google/genai', accept geminiApiKey as a parameter, instantiate GoogleGenAI with the key, build system prompt dynamically from timetable slots and tasks, and call ai.models.generateContent with model 'gemini-2.5-flash' and the user query plus conversation history. Return the AI-generated text response. This utility should handle API errors gracefully and return appropriate error messages.

Update the chat message sending flow to call the modified geminiChat utility with the user's geminiApiKey from UserContext. Remove any calls to sendMessage or other ICP actor methods for AI responses. Add conditional rendering: if geminiApiKey is absent, display an inline message prompting the user to add their key in the sidebar settings. Ensure the typing indicator shows during API calls, preserve the existing chat UI (scrollable history, Send button, Enter-to-send, Clear Chat button, formatted responses), and handle API errors by displaying them in the chat interface.

requirements.summary: Add @google/genai as a dependency in frontend/package.json to enable import resolution
acceptanceCriteria: @google/genai appears in the dependencies section of frontend/package.json
The frontend build completes without 'Cannot find module @google/genai' errors

file_operations.description: Add '@google/genai' to the dependencies section with an appropriate version (e.g., '^0.21.0' or latest stable). Ensure it is placed in the dependencies object, not devDependencies, so the import statement in Chat.tsx and geminiChat.ts resolves correctly during build and runtime.
